title: Freehand: tell me about your topic
checkLabels.t8: A list with at least three items
why.empty: An empty editor, again. And it is unpleasant, again. Do not think about the whole page — write just the heading. The rest follows it.
hint2.empty: One sentence. What is your page about? That is the <h1>.
why.not-closed: A tag is open and never closed, or closed in the wrong order. Read backwards from the end.
hint2.not-closed: Whatever you opened last, you close first.
hint3.not-closed:
Lists are the usual culprit. Every
  • wants its
    • , and the
      closes after the last of them.
why.no-h1: There is no single main heading. The page has to start with one thing that says what it is about. One — not two, not five. Put in more and the page shouts equally loudly about everything.
hint3.no-h1: The other headings do not disappear. They become <h2> — sections inside the page.
why.empty-h1: The heading is empty. An empty tag is not a heading.
why.no-h2: There are not two sections. The task asks for at least two, and a section without a name is not a section — it is just text that keeps going.
hint2.no-h2: What do you want to tell? Break it into parts. Each part wants a name.
hint3.no-h2: If your topic is a game: how I started, what I like, what I do not. Three sections, three <h2>.
why.empty-h2: There is an empty sub-heading. A section with no name is not a section.
why.no-p: There are not enough paragraphs. Headings with no text under them are a table of contents, not a page.
hint2.no-p: Under every heading there has to be something to read. At least one paragraph each.
why.empty-p: There is an empty paragraph. Either put text in it or remove it.
why.no-list: There is no list of at least three things. The task asks for one — reasons, favourites, steps, whatever fits.
hint2.no-list: If you wrote them with dashes inside a sentence, that is not a list. It looks like one. It is not.
hint3.no-list: <ul> on the outside, <li> around each thing. If the order means something — <ol>. If it does not — <ul>.
hint4.no-list: What in your topic can be counted? Reasons, steps, parts, favourites. List at least three.
why.empty-li: There is an empty item in the list. Either put text in it or remove it.
why.no-emphasis: There is not a single emphasis. In a whole page of text, something must weigh more than the rest. A warning, a key word, something that must not be missed.
hint2.no-emphasis: Read what you wrote out loud. Where would you raise your voice?
hint3.no-emphasis: <strong> — this is important. <em> — I am stressing this. One or the other, where it makes sense.
hint4.no-emphasis: Do not add emphasis just to pass the check. Find the place where it really weighs, and mark that.
blocks.2.text: Write a page about your topic that actually tells something. Not one line — a whole page someone would read to the end.
blocks.3.text: It must have: one main heading, at least two sections with sub-headings, at least three paragraphs, one list of at least three things (reasons, favourites, steps — whatever fits) and some emphasis where it makes sense.
blocks.4.text: I am not giving you a skeleton. I am not giving you an order. You decide what goes in and in what sequence.
blocks.6.text: It will look ugly. Serif font, everything in one column, no colours. You did nothing wrong — that is what a bare page without CSS looks like. The looks come in the next course.
blocks.7.text: Many people quit right here, because they think that if it is ugly, they must have made a mistake. You have not. You simply have not reached the beauty part yet.
